stories.headline: US and Iran trade fresh strikes & Snowflake's melt-up | The Pulse 5/28/2026
stories.summary:
The US and Iran traded fresh strikes. Oil prices climbed on fears of escalation and mixed signals on the status of peace talks. Meanwhile, software-maker Snowflake soared postmarket after signing a $6 billion chip and cloud deal with Amazon.

Today's guests: Greg Peters, PGIM Credit Co-CIO; Elizabeth Weymouth, Grafine Partners Founder and Managing Partner; Lorenzo Bini Smaghi, Former Societe Generale Chairman; Katie Gaddini, UCL Associate Profess

stories.headline: US Strikes Iran Targets; Snowflake Jumps on Results | Bloomberg Brief 5/28/2026
stories.summary: US equity futures fall and oil climbs after the US struck Iranian military targets for the second time this week. Snowflake surges after a stronger-than-expected annual outlook and signing a $6 billion deal with Amazon. Salesforce issues a tepid outlook. Michael Sneyd of BNP Paribas discusses Kevin Warsh's challenges as Fed Chair ahead of US PCE numbers. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Iran War: US Strikes Targets Near Hormuz With No Deal in Sight | The Opening Trade 5/28/2026
stories.summary:
Stocks fell while oil and bond yields climbed after fresh attacks in the Middle East fueled doubts over whether an end to the war is imminent.

S&P 500 futures slipped 0.2%, pointing to the benchmark’s first decline in more than a week. Brent rose 2.9% toward $97 a barrel after the US carried out airstrikes on an Iranian military site, clouding hopes for a diplomatic breakthrough.

stories.headline: Ambani Says Reliance Evaluating ‘Strategic Pathways’ for Jio, No Word on IPO
stories.summary: Reliance Industries Ltd. is taking “deliberate steps” to strengthen its digital unit, Chairman Mukesh Ambani said in a note to shareholders, without sharing any updates on the planned listing of Jio Platforms Ltd. that is expected to fetch as much as $4 billion.

stories.headline: BCI Expands Into Financing Private Equity Funds Amid Deal Slump
stories.summary: British Columbia Investment Management Corp. created a team within its private equity unit to provide financing to buyout firms that are increasingly looking for new ways to drum up cash amid a prolonged dealmaking drought.

stories.headline: AI’s Grip on Emerging Markets Fuels Rise in Stock-Picking ETFs
stories.summary: Large asset managers are rolling out a wave of actively managed emerging-market ETFs, pitching them as alternatives to benchmarks increasingly dominated by AI stocks.

stories.headline: Bob Doll Says Stocks Are in a ‘High-Risk Bull Market’
stories.summary: Bob Doll, CIO at Crossmark Global Investments, says his firm is trying to be “more reactionary than usual” in the current market environment and is looking to buy stocks with “okay fundamentals.” (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: AT1 Market Is So Hot That Banks Are Locking In Rates for Decade
stories.summary: European lenders are selling Additional Tier 1 bonds with long call dates at a record pace, seizing ultra-favorable conditions in the market for risky bank debt to fix costs for a decade.

stories.headline: Nomura Raises Annual Profit Target 50% After Record Year

stories.headline: UK Could Miss Housing Target by 40% or More, Research Finds

stories.headline: Korea’s Pension Fund Raises Domestic Stock Target as Kospi Soars
stories.summary: South Korea’s giant pension fund sharply ramped up its allocation target for domestic equities, a move that may help it avoid forced sales of Kospi shares after the recent rally pushed its holdings above the limit.
